panels.title: Daily peak vs capacity floor (kW)
panels.description: Per calendar day (Brussels): the MAX of 15-minute mean grid power — the Fluvius capacity-tariff billing basis. Bars are coloured by the 2.5 kW capacity floor (green below, red above, dashed red threshold line). The purple dashed line is zeus's own running monthly capacity peak (capacity_peak_kw, resets each month); it may diverge slightly from the recomputed daily max because zeus aligns quarter-hours to its own cycle.

targets.query:
import "timezone"
option location = timezone.location(name: "Europe/Brussels")
hist = from(bucket: "zeus") |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop) |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "zeus_grid_power_w" and r._field == "value")
live = from(bucket: "zeus") |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop) |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "zeus_state" and r._field == "grid_power_w")
union(tables: [hist, live])
  |> group()
  |> aggregateWindow(every: 15m, fn: mean, createEmpty: false)
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({ r with _value: r._value / 1000.0 }))
  |> aggregateWindow(every: 1d, fn: max, createEmpty: false)
  |> keep(columns: ["_time", "_value"])
  |> rename(columns: {_value: "Daily peak (kW)"})
  |> sort(columns: ["_time"])

import "timezone"
option location = timezone.location(name: "Europe/Brussels")
hist = from(bucket: "zeus") |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop) |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "zeus_capacity_peak_kw" and r._field == "value")
live = from(bucket: "zeus") |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop) |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "zeus_state" and r._field == "capacity_peak_kw")
union(tables: [hist, live])
  |> group()
  |> aggregateWindow(every: 1d, fn: max, createEmpty: false)
  |> keep(columns: ["_time", "_value"])
  |> rename(columns: {_value: "Running monthly peak (kW)"})
  |> sort(columns: ["_time"])

panels.title: Top-10 peak events (30d)
panels.description: The 10 highest 15-minute mean grid draws of the last 30 days, with battery state at that moment: SoC and mode (grid/SoC are 15-min means, mode is the last mode in the window). Red background ≥ 3.5 kW, orange ≥ 2.5 kW (above the capacity floor), green below.

targets.query:
import "timezone"
option location = timezone.location(name: "Europe/Brussels")
base = from(bucket: "zeus")
  |> range(start: -30d)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "zeus_state")
gs = base
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._field == "grid_power_w" or r._field == "soc_pct")
  |> aggregateWindow(every: 15m, fn: mean, createEmpty: false)
md = base
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._field == "mode_code")
  |> aggregateWindow(every: 15m, fn: last, createEmpty: false)
union(tables: [gs, md])
  |> pivot(rowKey: ["_time"], columnKey: ["_field"], valueColumn: "_value")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => exists r.grid_power_w)
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({
      _time: r._time,
      kW: r.grid_power_w / 1000.0,
      soc: r.soc_pct,
      mode: r.mode_code
    }))
  |> group()
  |> top(n: 10, columns: ["kW"])
  |> sort(columns: ["kW"], desc: true)
  |> keep(columns: ["_time", "kW", "soc", "mode"])
  |> rename(columns: {_time: "Time", kW: "Draw (kW)", soc: "SoC (%)", mode: "Mode"})

panels.title: Cost heatmap — weekday × hour (avg €/h, 30d)
panels.description: Average hourly import COST (€/h) per weekday × hour over the last 30 days (Brussels). Per hour: kWh = mean grid power / 1000 × 1 h, cost = kWh × mean import price for that hour; cells average those hourly costs per (weekday, hour). Green = cheap, red = expensive — hover a cell for the exact value. Slightly negative cells are possible (negative prices).

targets.query:
import "date"
import "timezone"
option location = timezone.location(name: "Europe/Brussels")
from(bucket: "zeus")
  |> range(start: -30d)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "zeus_state" and (r._field == "grid_power_w" or r._field == "import_price_eur"))
  |> aggregateWindow(every: 1h, fn: mean, createEmpty: false)
  |> pivot(rowKey: ["_time"], columnKey: ["_field"], valueColumn: "_value")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => exists r.grid_power_w and exists r.import_price_eur)
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({ r with cost: r.grid_power_w / 1000.0 * r.import_price_eur }))
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({ r with
       wd:   (if date.weekDay(t: r._time) == 1 then "1 Mon" else if date.weekDay(t: r._time) == 2 then "2 Tue" else if date.weekDay(t: r._time) == 3 then "3 Wed" else if date.weekDay(t: r._time) == 4 then "4 Thu" else if date.weekDay(t: r._time) == 5 then "5 Fri" else if date.weekDay(t: r._time) == 6 then "6 Sat" else "7 Sun"),
       hour: (if date.hour(t: r._time) < 10 then "0" else "") + string(v: date.hour(t: r._time)) }))
  |> group(columns: ["wd", "hour"])
  |> mean(column: "cost")
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({ weekday: r.wd, hour: r.hour, eur: r.cost }))
  |> group()
  |> sort(columns: ["hour", "weekday"])
  |> keep(columns: ["weekday", "hour", "eur"])

panels.title: Load duration curve (30d)
panels.description: Every 15-minute mean grid power sample of the last 30 days, sorted descending: x = hours at or above that load (rank × 0.25 h), y = kW. A steep left spike = short peaks (batterable); a high flat plateau = sustained load. Dashed red line = 2.5 kW capacity-tariff floor.

targets.query:
import "timezone"
option location = timezone.location(name: "Europe/Brussels")
hist = from(bucket: "zeus") |> range(start: -30d) |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "zeus_grid_power_w" and r._field == "value")
live = from(bucket: "zeus") |> range(start: -30d) |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "zeus_state" and r._field == "grid_power_w")
union(tables: [hist, live])
  |> group()
  |> aggregateWindow(every: 15m, fn: mean, createEmpty: false)
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({ kW: r._value / 1000.0 }))
  |> group()
  |> sort(columns: ["kW"], desc: true)
  |> keep(columns: ["kW"])

panels.title: Daily consumption & cost (30d)
panels.description: Per calendar day (Brussels), last 30 days: total energy imported from the grid (bars, kWh, left axis) and what it cost at the dynamic import price (yellow line, €, right axis). Energy: hourly mean grid power → kWh, summed per day. Cost: per hour kWh × mean import price, summed per day. Cheap-price days show a low € line even at high kWh.

targets.query:
import "timezone"
option location = timezone.location(name: "Europe/Brussels")
from(bucket: "zeus")
  |> range(start: -30d)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "zeus_state" and r._field == "grid_power_w")
  |> aggregateWindow(every: 1h, fn: mean, createEmpty: false)
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({ r with _value: r._value / 1000.0 }))
  |> aggregateWindow(every: 1d, fn: sum, createEmpty: false)
  |> keep(columns: ["_time", "_value"])
  |> rename(columns: {_value: "Consumption (kWh)"})
  |> sort(columns: ["_time"])

import "timezone"
option location = timezone.location(name: "Europe/Brussels")
from(bucket: "zeus")
  |> range(start: -30d)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "zeus_state" and (r._field == "grid_power_w" or r._field == "import_price_eur"))
  |> aggregateWindow(every: 1h, fn: mean, createEmpty: false)
  |> pivot(rowKey: ["_time"], columnKey: ["_field"], valueColumn: "_value")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => exists r.grid_power_w and exists r.import_price_eur)
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({ r with _value: r.grid_power_w / 1000.0 * r.import_price_eur }))
  |> aggregateWindow(every: 1d, fn: sum, createEmpty: false)
  |> keep(columns: ["_time", "_value"])
  |> rename(columns: {_value: "Cost (EUR)"})
  |> sort(columns: ["_time"])
